commands.review_summary:
The PR derives text-slash command metadata for authorized Telegram generic-message control commands and adds a regression test for `/compact` when native/text command toggles are disabled.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +31. Total +35 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction in this review. Source inspection shows current main leaves generic Telegram message ingress without `commandSource`, and the PR adds a harness regression for that path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Do not queue a repair; the remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer live Telegram proof, not a code change ClawSweeper can safely synthesize on the branch.

target.title: fix(telegram): restore /compact on generic message ingress
target.body:
## Summary

Telegram messages that reach the generic `bot.on("message")` path (instead of `bot.command`) did not set `commandSource`, so authorized control commands like `/compact` were built without `text-slash` command metadata and were silently dropped (no `commands.log` entry). This mirrors Mattermost’s `CommandSource: "text"` classification for authorized control commands.

- Derive `commandSource: "text"` in `buildTelegramInboundContextPayload` when `commandAuthorized && hasControlCommand` and no explicit source was passed.
- Add regression test covering generic-path `/compact` with `commands.native: false` and `commands.text: false`.

Fixes #89525

## Verification

```bash
pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts -t "routes generic-path control commands"
```

```
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  98 passed (98)
```

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram `/compact` (and other control commands on generic message ingress) produced no reply and no `commands.log` entry after upgrade; root cause was missing `commandSource` / `text-slash` command classification on the generic path.
- Real environment tested: macOS, Node 22.16, local OpenClaw checkout (`fix/telegram-compact-command-source`), Vitest telegram extension suite.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts -t "routes generic-path control commands"`
- Evidence after fix: Vitest regression asserts `replySpy` receives `CommandSource: "text"` and `CommandTurn.kind: "text-slash"` for DM `/compact` on generic ingress with native commands disabled.
- Observed result after fix: Test passes; inbound context now carries text-slash command metadata required for `shouldHandleTextCommands` when `commands.text: false` on a native-command surface.
- What was not tested: Live Telegram client roundtrip on Raspberry Pi / production gateway (no Crabbox Telegram session in this run).
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Proof is harness-level command-metadata regression, not a live Telegram screenshot. Maintainer may want Crabbox/Telegram live repro per issue label.

## Risks and mitigations

- Risk: Misclassifying non-command text as control commands — mitigated by `hasControlCommand` + `commandAuthorized` gates (same invariant as Mattermost).
- Risk: Double-handling when native `bot.command` also fires — unchanged; explicit `options.commandSource` still wins.

— [Joel Nishanth](https://offlyn.ai) · [offlyn.AI](https://offlyn.ai)

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
target.author: joelnishanth
